subject: Geology
title: Hot Springs and Geysers
description: A 60-second lesson on Hot Springs and Geysers.

hook.text: Old Faithful erupts almost every 90 minutes. Underground water meets hot rock and pressure builds until it blows.

buildup.text: Hot springs form where groundwater is heated by magma or hot rock. Geysers erupt when trapped steam builds pressure.

discovery.text: Yellowstone sits on a supervolcano. Its hot springs and geysers are powered by a massive magma chamber below.

twist.text: Hot spring minerals create colorful terraces and pools. Grand Prismatic Spring gets its rainbow from bacteria.

climax.text: Hot springs and geysers are windows into geothermal activity. They show the heat engine running beneath the surface.

punchline.text: Boiling water reveals the fire below.

quiz.question: What causes a geyser to erupt?
options: Trapped steam from heated groundwater builds until released
Earthquakes open cracks in the ground
